studies.title: Randomised controlled trial to evaluate the App-based Multimodal Obesity-management programme “Oviva Direkt für Adipositas” - AMODA
studies.health_condition:
ICD-10-GM (translation):
E66 - Obesity
Healthy volunteers:
No
studies.intervention:
Arm 1:
Intervention group: Intervention with “Oviva Direkt für Adipositas” for 48 weeks
Arm 2:
Control group: Standard of care for 24 weeks followed by intervention with “Oviva Direkt für Adipositas” for 24 weeks
Endpoints

studies.title: Randomised, controlled trial to evaluate the effectiveness of Oviva Direkt in reducing blood pressure in patients with hypertension
studies.health_condition:
ICD-10-GM (translation):
I10 - Essential (primary) hypertension
Healthy volunteers:
No
studies.brief_summary:
Hypertension is the major preventable cause of cardiovascular disease and all-cause mortality globally and in Europe. 30-45% of all patients in 2015 showed hypertension, making hypertension control a worldwide challenge. Lifestyle changes like healthy eating, body weight reduction, regular exercise, and stress management help patients to lower their blood pressure. However, it is important to maintain these changes even after therapy has ended. 
Mobile phone apps (digital therapeutics) are a new approach to facilitate the necessary lifestyle modifications and in Germany they are even reimbursed as Digitale Gesundheitsanwendungen (DiGA). The evidence to support the effectiveness of DiGAs to control hypertension in Germany is growing. As a european provider for digital therapeutics, Oviva now launched a DiGA for hypertension control - Oviva Direkt Hypertension. Oviva is well renowned for digitally-enabled programmes to support sustainable healthy lifestyle changes through behaviour chang
studies.intervention:
Arm 1:
Intervention group: Oviva Direkt Hypertension (App-based digital 
lifestyle intervention) + standard of care
Arm 2:
Comparison group: standard of care
Endpoints

studies.title: A Randomised Controlled Trial of Oviva Direkt for Improvement of Glycaemic Control in Patients Living with Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus (T2DM)
studies.health_condition:
ICD-10-GM (translation):
E11 - Type 2 diabetes mellitus
Healthy volunteers:
No
studies.brief_summary:
In this clinical study, the effectiveness of an app-based lifestyle intervention called Oviva Direkt Diabetes Type 2 will be investigated. 
There will be two groups, one of which will participate in the Oviva Direkt Diabetes Type 2 programme (intervention group), while the other group will receive no additional treatment within the study (control group). The results of the two groups after 24 weeks of participation in the study will be compared. The main measurement will be the HbA1c result. All participants will continue to receive their regular care, independently from their participation in the study.
studies.intervention:
Arm 1:
Oviva Direkt Diabetes Typ 2 (app-based digital lifestyle intervention) + care as usual for 24 weeks
Arm 2:
care as usual for 24 weeks
Endpoints
